evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: setWebhook with secret_token, X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header, sendMessage, answerCallbackQuery, inline_keyboard structure all match Telegram Bot API conventions. No version claim but self-consistent.
evidence.executability: Python code is syntactically valid with proper imports. However, testing procedure mandates ngrok (sandbox-infeasibility pattern), which caps EXECUTABILITY at 2.
evidence.completeness: (a) acquisition steps present, (b) pip install in bash, (c) .env template with TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_SECRET_TOKEN matching code references, (d) backend code present, (e) no frontend (N/A for Telegram bot), (f) test is curl + manual Telegram interaction rather than automated runnable command.
evidence.citation_anchoring: No official docs URLs cited (core.telegram.org/bots/api missing). Zero citations.

rubric_notes: Telegram bot has no frontend so piece (e) is N/A. ngrok requirement caps executability at 2 per harness rule.
